fault_hypothesis: Lines 74-75 incorrectly remove the last element when both inputs share the same final value, which is not a valid deduplication step for a merge — the merged result should always contain all elements from both inputs.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for a correct merge; it incorrectly removes a valid duplicate element.
After initialization, merged must be empty before any elements are added.
The then-branch of the spurious condition at line 74 should never execute in a correct merge implementation.
The merged result must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
